rationale: Both evaluations land on an identical 3/10 score and share the same core reasoning (no Director-level IC scope, no shipped AI products, limited cloud depth), which argues for justified. However, the only resume change was employer names, and the variant quietly drops the baseline's 'Founder shows entrepreneurial drive' strength and instead manufactures a new concern about lacking an 'entrepreneurial-within-large-org track record' — a framing that keys off the changed Founder employer (GISCollective→MercadoLibre) rather than any change in the candidate's actual work. The experience-estimate drift (13-14 vs ~15 years) is within ordinary run-to-run noise. Since the score is unaffected but one reasoning shift attaches to the employer change, this is mixed.
